---
title: "Modernization Canon"
subtitle: "Microsoft-Driven Identity & Infrastructure Migration"
---

## What This Is

Federal agencies are frozen in 2003. Active Directory was not just an identity
store — it was the implicit governance model for every network service in the
enterprise. Microsoft is deprecating the entire Client/Server stack, but nobody
gave agencies a framework for what decisions to make with the new tools.

This modernization canon is that framework.

## Two Domains, One Landing Zone

| Domain | Namespace | Focus |
|--------|-----------|-------|
| **Identity Modernization** | MOD_xxx | OrgPath hierarchy, dynamic groups, HR-driven lifecycle, x.500-to-flat mapping |
| **Directory Migration** | DM_xxx | DNS, PKI, RADIUS, LDAP, devices, file services |

- [Identity Modernization (OrgTree)](orgtree.qmd)
- [Directory Migration](directory-migration.qmd)

### Canon Rule

> uiao-gos is a universal enterprise product. It is NOT government-specific.
> Federal compliance (FedRAMP, OSCAL, KSI) is one vertical adapter on top of
> the universal core. Never frame the core engine as federal-only.
